memoryArtifacts.headline: Inquisitions Post Mortem, Edward I, File 38 517. Hugh Aguillon alias Agulun. Writ, 5 May, 12 Edw. I.
memoryArtifacts.description:
Inquisitions Post Mortem, Edward I, File 38
517.	Hugh Aguillon alias Agulun.
Writ, 5 May, 12 Edw. I.
Warwick.	Inq. Monday before the Ascension, 12 Edw. I.
Upton by Haselouer. A manse with dovecot and 4 virgates land, 1 virgate with a messuage in villenage, 4 cottages, a grove, a water-mill, and 10d. rent from two free tenants (names given), held of John son and heir of William le Botiler of Wemme, of the fee of the earl of Warwick, who is under age and in the king's wardship, by service of 1 knight.
Stodleye. 6s rent from two freemen holding 2 messuages.
Ellen sometime his wife claims dower of all the above.
He died on Saturday the eve of the close of Easter, 12 Edw. I.
He had four sisters, of whom Christiana the eldest is dead, and her son and heir named Hugh Trenchevent is aged 60 and more; another sister named Hawis is also dead, whose son named William de Whitenton is aged 40 and more; the third sister named Joan still survives and is aged 80 and more; and the fourth sister named Maud is dead and her son John is aged 26 and more: the said Hugh, William, Joan and John are his next heirs.
C. Edw. I. File 38. (1.)

zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Aguillon於1205年出生在英國素福爾可時，她的父親Robert de Aguillon20歲，母親Margery Fresney23歲。她於大約1226年與Lord Ralph Fitzbernard Kingsdowne在英國英格蘭肯特金斯堂結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1263年在英國英格蘭肯特享年58歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾。

nameMeanings.name: Aguillon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Jesus, Pablo, Juan, Agustin, Carlos, Ernesto, Manuel, Roberto, Ruben, Santiago.
habitational name from any of several places called Aguillón in Spain (Asturias or Galicia), or Aguillon in Var, France.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Aguillon was born in 1205, in Suffolk, England, her father, Robert de Aguillon, was 20 and her mother, Margery Fresney, was 23. She married Lord Ralph Fitzbernard Kingsdowne about 1226, in Kingsdown, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1263, in Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 58, and was buried in Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Aguillon est née en 1205, à Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Aguillon, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Margery Fresney, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé Lord Ralph Fitzbernard Kingsdowne environ 1226, à Kingsdown, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1263, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterrée à Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Aguillon(이)가 1205년 영국 수폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Aguillon(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margery Fresney(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 대략 1226년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 킨그즈다운에서 Lord Ralph Fitzbernard Kingsdowne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1263년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Aguillon nasceu em 1205, em Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Aguillon, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Margery Fresney, tinha 23 anos. Ela casou-se com Lord Ralph Fitzbernard Kingsdowne aproximadamente 1226, em Kingsdown, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1263, em Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 58 anos, e foi sepultada em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.Kingsdown St John, Kent, England, United: кингсдоун ст джон, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Aguillon родилась в 1205 г. в местности суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert de Aguillon было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Margery Fresney, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Lord Ralph Fitzbernard Kingsdowne около 1226 г. в местности кингсдоун, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1263 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 58 лет и была похоронена в местности глоусестиршир, Англия.
